name: Cherokee Succotash
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Fresh or dry Lima beans
3 cups Fresh corn cut from cob
recipeInstructions:
1. If using dry beans, soak them for 3-4 hours.
2. Bring the water to a boil.
3. Add the beans and cook at a moderate boil for 10 minutes.
4. Add the corn, ham hocks, salt, pepper, and onions.
5. Reduce heat and simmer on low heat for 1 hour.
Note: This recipe was shared with me by a Cherokee Amerindian cultural anthropologist. He assures me it is genuinely ethnic to his people in North Carolina. The starred items () were provided by him, and the measurements have been converted for modern use. Enjoy!
